SEARCH
Package com.github.dreamteam.controllers

Class BaseController

java.lang.Object
com.github.dreamteam.controllers.BaseController
@RestController @RequestMapping("/") public class BaseController extends Object
BaseController serves as a gateway for interacting with the ML component. It provides endpoints for initializing, cleaning, and training ML models.

  • Constructor Summary

    Description

  • Constructor Details

    • BaseController

      public BaseController()

  • Method Details

    • index

      @RequestMapping("/") public String index()
      Root endpoint to verify service availability.
      Returns:
      a greeting message.

    • initialize

      @PostMapping("/init") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<String> initialize()
      Initializes the ML system by first cleaning data and then training a model and making a prediction. This is a sequential process where each step depends on the success of the previous one.
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity indicating success or failure.

    • initializeWithMotivation

      @PostMapping("/initWithMotivation") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<String> initializeWithMotivation()
      Initializes the ML system including motivation scoring. This is a sequential process where each step depends on the success of the previous one.
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity indicating success or failure.

    • cleanData

      @PostMapping("/clean") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<String> cleanData(@RequestParam(required=false) Optional<String> data, @RequestParam(required=false) Optional<String> dataCleaner, @RequestParam(required=false) Optional<String> saveFile)
      Cleans the data specified by the user. It can take a data file, a cleaner, and a save file as optional parameters. The cleaner is a specific data cleaning method from the /data_handling endpoint.
      Parameters:
      data - The data file to be cleaned.
      dataCleaner - The cleaner to be used for data cleaning.
      saveFile - The name of the file where the cleaned data will be saved.
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity indicating success or failure.

    • trainModel

      @PostMapping("/train") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<String> trainModel(@RequestParam(required=false) Optional<String> modelType, @RequestParam(required=false) Optional<String> modelName, @RequestParam(required=false) Optional<String> data, @RequestParam(required=false) Optional<Boolean> cleaning)
      Trains a model based on the specified parameters. It can take a model type, model name, data, and a cleaning flag as optional parameters.
      Parameters:
      modelType - The type of model to be trained.
      modelName - The name of the model to be trained.
      data - The data file to be used for training.
      cleaning - A flag indicating whether cleaning is needed.
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity indicating success or failure.

    • trainAllModels

      @PostMapping("/train/all") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<String> trainAllModels()
      Placeholder for training all available ML models.
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity indicating that the functionality is not implemented.

    • cleanAllData

      @PostMapping("/clean/all") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<String> cleanAllData()
      Placeholder for cleaning datasets using all available cleaners.
      Returns:
      ResponseEntity indicating that the functionality is not implemented.
